description: Authoritative source-of-truth for top-level model IDs referenced by peer-spawn.js. Curated manually by the operator. Drift between the constants pinned in src/lib/peer-spawn.js and the entries here triggers ERROR in scripts/audit-model-drift.js. fallback_chain[0] MUST equal id (spec v4.8 section 6.9.3 invariant). last_verified staleness beyond staleness_threshold_days triggers WARN (forcing a cadence of human revalidation). This file is advisory-only per workflow-spec v4.3 section 6.9.2.1: it does NOT drive runtime model selection. Changes to runtime model IDs still require bump + spec edit per section 6.9.2. Schema v2 introduced in v0.5.0-alpha (triangular + resilience).

codex.notes_en: Top-tier OpenAI Codex model at last_verified. Operator is on highest OpenAI subscription tier (see memory user_subscription_tier.md); no gating. Codex CLI errors cleanly on nonexistent IDs (no silent downgrade).

claude.notes_en: Full ID, not alias, per section 6.9.2 auditability clause. Top-tier Anthropic model at last_verified. Operator is on highest Anthropic subscription tier.

gemini.notes_en: Gemini CLI 0.39.1 silently downgrades on nonexistent IDs: e.g., `-m gemini-3.1-pro-preview` accepted but responds as gemini-2.5-pro; `-m gemini-3-pro-preview` responds as gemini-2.5-flash. `-m gemini-3-pro` / `-m gemini-pro-latest` return 404. Pin is the verified top available on the operator's oauth-personal auth path. 3.x previews are still in beta on this auth path per 2026-04-24 operator directive; revisit pin on GA. v0.5.0-alpha adds a silent-downgrade enforcement layer in peer-spawn.js (TODO-spec-v4.9) so runtime fails hard rather than silently downgrades.
